ddls:
CREATE TABLE "devices" (
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each device model */,
  "model_name" TEXT /* Name of the device model */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the device model */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the device (e.g., router, modem, etc.) */,
  "subcategory" TEXT /* Subcategory of the device (e.g., dual-band, single-band, etc.) */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Manufacturer of the device */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Release date of the device */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "specifications" (
  "spec_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each specification */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device model the specification belongs to */,
  "parameter" TEXT /* Name of the specification parameter */,
  "min_value" REAL /* Minimum value of the specification */,
  "typical_value" REAL /* Typical value of the specification */,
  "max_value" REAL /* Maximum value of the specification */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the specification */,
  "tolerance" TEXT /* Tolerance of the specification (e.g., ±5%, ±10%, etc.) */,
  "upper_limit" REAL /* Upper limit of the specification */,
  "lower_limit" REAL /* Lower limit of the specification */,
  "target_value" REAL /* Target value of the specification */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("spec_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_specifications_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_specifications_unit FOREIGN KEY ("unit") REFERENCES units ("unit_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_specifications_parameter FOREIGN KEY ("parameter") REFERENCES parameters ("param_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "parameters" (
  "param_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each parameter */,
  "param_name" TEXT /* Name of the parameter */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the parameter */,
  "unit_type" TEXT /* Unit type of the parameter (e.g., frequency, impedance, etc.) */,
  "data_type" TEXT /* Data type of the parameter (e.g., float, integer, etc.) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("param_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "units" (
  "unit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each unit */,
  "unit_name" TEXT /* Name of the unit */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the unit */,
  "symbol" TEXT /* Symbol of the unit (e.g., Hz, Ω, etc.) */,
  "conversion_factor" TEXT /* Conversion factor of the unit (e.g., 1 Hz = 1/s, etc.) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("unit_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "device_models" (
  "model_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each device model */,
  "model_name" TEXT /* Name of the device model */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the device model */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, engineer, QA) */,
  "dept_id" INTEGER /* Department ID of the user */,
  "dept_role" TEXT /* Role of the user within the department */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_dept_id FOREIGN KEY ("dept_id") REFERENCES departments ("dept_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device model being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the device model */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the device model was accessed */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Time when the device model was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit, delete, etc.) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "specification_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each change event */,
  "spec_id" INTEGER /* ID of the specification that was changed */,
  "change_date" TEXT /* Date when the change was made */,
  "change_time" TEXT /* Time when the change was made */,
  "change_type" TEXT /* Type of change (e.g., update, insert, delete, etc.) */,
  "change_description" TEXT /* Description of the change */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_specification_history_spec_id FOREIGN KEY ("spec_id") REFERENCES specifications ("spec_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "departments" (
  "dept_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each department */,
  "dept_name" TEXT /* Name of the department */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the department */,
  "manager_id" INTEGER /* Manager ID of the department */,
  "dept_description" TEXT /* Description of the department */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("dept_id")
)
